title: Extract and Save Sender's Email Address from Outlook Email
excerpt:
Hello - I have integrated my monday board with Outlook and creating a new item when an email is received. I would like to extract the sender’s email address and save it into an email or text column. Is that possible? 
Th…

posts.author: HarjotB (Harjot)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello - I have integrated my monday board with Outlook and creating a new item when an email is received. I would like to extract the sender’s email address and save it into an email or text column. Is that possible?

Thanks

posts.author: peta (Peta Bolger)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @HarjotB

Welcome to the Community!

You might find a 3rd party app can help you bridge that gap. Have a search for ‘extract’ in the app marketplace and try Extract AI app. It provides a number of recipes to allow use cases like yours to operate smoothly.

An example:

Give it a try and let me know if you can’t find a suitable automation!

posts.author: HarjotB (Harjot)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you @peta for your response. I was hoping for a native solution.
App is a bit expensive for the customer. I will probably try this out using Zapier or Make.com and report back.

Thank you again.

posts.author: henrymurphy (Henry Murphy)
posts.cookedHtml: There are many times when Outlook users need to extract the sender address of an email and save it. I have two methods here—a manual method and an automated tool-based approach. Both are simple and depend on your need. If you are doing this for only a few emails, then the manual method works well. But if you want to extract and save sender email addresses from multiple emails automatically, then you can use a tool. So I recommend you use a professional tool like Softaken Email Address Extractor to extract attachments from emails. It also comes with a free demo version, so you can try the program before purchasing.
